MOTION TO CONTINUE JURY TRIAL
Off Calendar Apr-22-2025 Text Ruling

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To Amend The Complaint
Off Calendar Feb-18-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (by Zoom):
Melanie Proctor, Esq. 415-262-0096 THE OTTINGER FIRM, P.C.for Plaintiff.
Jennifer Terry, Esq. 213-457-8000 Reed Smith for Defendant.

ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF ETHAN SUTIN's Motion For Leave To Amend The Complaint.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. In addition, the ex parte application for an order shortening time/briefing schedule for Defendant's motion for summary judgment/summary adjudication is off-calendar as moot in light of today's ruling.

Plaintiff Ethan Sutin's motion for leave to amend the complaint is granted conditioned on Mr. Sutin stipulating to a new trial date of November 10, 2025 or any other date mutually agreeable to the parties and joining in an application to request that date from the master calendar department. If Mr. Sutin refuses to accept the condition, the motion is denied. Mr. Sutin may express his acceptance of the condition in a proposed order reflecting the tentative ruling. The correspondence between counsel submitted with the reply papers show that defendant X Corp. believes that its claimed prejudice from the granting of this motion would be eliminated by a trial continuance of 90 days, but that counsel were unable to agree on dates 90 or later than the current trial date due to scheduling conflicts and Mr. Sutin's unwillingness to set a trial date in November. Given the delayed seeking of permission to amend by Mr. Sutin, his refusal to consider a trial date in November is not reasonable.

Order is signed.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/HEK)
